purpose: Specifies how Synapse commands choose which dashboard to act on. Defines two mechanisms: claiming (for write ops via priority chain: pre-assigned DASHBOARD ID directive > --dashboard flag > ask user) and detection (for read ops, defaulting to the assigned dashboard). Critically establishes mandatory dashboard isolation -- agents have NO read or write access to dashboards other than their assigned one.
gotchas: There is NO auto-selection or scanning -- agents must have an assigned dashboard or ask the user
When the assigned dashboard already has data, the agent must ASK the user before archiving and reusing -- never proceed silently
Pre-assigned dashboards (from system prompt DASHBOARD ID directive) are always authoritative; --dashboard flag is secondary
Each chat view in the Electron app is bound to exactly one dashboard for isolation
patterns: Priority-chain selection with explicit user prompts on conflict
conventions: First positional argument matching 6-char hex format is parsed as dashboardId; task IDs use N.N format; flags use --
Worker dispatch prompts always include {dashboardId} alongside {tracker_root} and {project_root}
